Chromium Daily Summaries

Latest Chromium commits summarized daily

📅 View Weekly Summaries →

2026-03-23

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-23

Overview

This summary covers 578 commits on the main branch for March 23, 2026, with 438 identified as relevant to developers building on Chromium. The day was marked by an unusually high number of breaking changes across core APIs, networking, and UI components, requiring significant updates for embedders. Key non-breaking work included progress on post-quantum cryptography and UI enhancements for Vertical Tabs. The first relevant commit was (12284ce) and the last was (db815f5).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API in ui/base/clipboard/clipboard.h has been modified. Methods like WriteBookmark() and ReadBookmark() have been renamed to WriteURL() and ReadUrl() respectively. Embedders using the clipboard for URL/bookmark data must update their method calls. (624e217)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Multiple core API signatures have been changed, requiring code updates. This includes: the removal of the 2-parameter overload for content::WebContents::IgnoreInputEvents; renaming OnComplete() to OnBannerShown() in webapps::AppBannerManager::Observer; adding a force_to_proceed parameter to the BeforeUnload Mojo API; and changing Browser* parameters to BrowserWindowInterface* in chrome/browser/ui/browser_commands.h. (05268bd) (e89a8d0) (2cb8be8) (14ee99d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The AccountSettingService has been moved from components/autofill to a new components/account_settings component. This changes its namespace from autofill to account_settings, breaking includes and direct references. (a1b05c5) (5c162c5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core networking Mojo interface network.mojom.NetworkContext.CreateWebSocket has a new network_restrictions_id parameter. Direct callers must be updated to provide this new value. (0f1662a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Navigation queueing is now permanently enabled, and the kQueueNavigationsWhileWaitingForCommit feature flag has been removed. This represents a fundamental behavioral change in how concurrent navigations are handled. The UndoCommitNavigation() IPC method was also removed from content/common/frame.mojom. (369b2d9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several public APIs and components have been refactored or removed. This includes: renaming OnTabsClosed to OnNodesClosed in TabStripService; removing the PageActionIconType::kCollaborationMessaging enum; removing GetOnDeviceCapabilities from the OnDeviceCapability class; and changing the constructor for components/sync_device_info/DeviceInfo. (73c9765) (2c4a110) (6e00555) (505ebd5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING File paths for key resources have been changed, which will break builds with hardcoded include paths. x509_certificate_model.h moved from chrome/common/net to components/certificate_model, and cws_item_service.proto moved from //chrome to //extensions. (35ed0b5) (74dc059)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The command-line switch --enable-webgl-image-chromium has been renamed to --enable-webgl-shared-image. Scripts and configurations using the old switch must be updated. (1d5f025)

APIs & Core

  • A new LocationIconInterface has been introduced to decouple location bar chip controllers from the native Views implementation. This abstraction will enable future WebUI-based location bar components. (8222d0b)
  • As part of Project Bedrock, the ProfileManager's dependency on BrowserListObserver has been removed to reduce coupling with the Browser object. (7427fd2)
  • A new SidePanelUIProvider class provides a cross-platform way to retrieve the SidePanelUI interface, simplifying code that interacts with the side panel. (dc6cacd)
  • Navigation-related protocol buffers have been moved from session_specifics.proto to a new tab_navigation.proto. This refactoring enables reuse for features like Send-Tab-To-Self history without depending on the entire Sessions data type. (84f565f)

Networking & Security

  • Experimental support for the post-quantum signature algorithm ML-DSA (variants 44, 65, and 87) has been added to WebCrypto. The feature is gated by the WebCryptoPQC runtime flag. (2da14fd)
  • A new enterprise policy, MaxConnectionsPerProxyForWebSocket, has been added. This allows administrators to control the WebSocket proxy connection limit, providing more granular control over network resources. (1c530cb)
  • The Brotli compression library was updated to fix a decoding hang on certain inputs. (224140d)
  • A bug in the file download access prevention mechanism that could cause downloads of highly compressible files to fail has been fixed. (81d22a5)

Browser UI (Windows/macOS)

  • The Vertical Tabs feature continues to be refined, with improvements to focus management after moving tabs, a fix for the drop indicator arrow's position, and the ability for the tab strip region to expand on hover. (5d9dd66) (15a6396) (885030e)
  • A crash on macOS in TabStrip::IsRectInWindowCaption that could occur when switching to vertical tabs has been fixed by adding a null check. (703c279)
  • Crashes in the Profile Picker and other signed-out flows were fixed by ensuring WebContentsModalDialogManager is correctly initialized for the associated WebContents. (961e217) (6d519a6)
  • Payment Handler windows will now respect the "Auto-open DevTools for pop-ups" setting, improving the developer experience for debugging payment flows. (6b7477f)

Build & Test Infrastructure

  • The autotest script was improved with a new --run-related flag to heuristically find and run tests related to locally modified files, simplifying the developer workflow. (7d04f66) (0ee936d) (df2610b)
  • The linux-afl-asan-rel builder, which has been broken for over a year, was removed from the build infrastructure, along with its associated afl GN config. (45a34c1)
  • The Siso build tool was updated to version 1.5.7. (32939b2)

2026-03-22

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-22

Overview

This digest covers 21 commits from the Chromium repository. After filtering for changes relevant to desktop embedders (Windows/macOS) and excluding platform-specific, localization, and minor changes, this report focuses on significant updates to the browser's core UI, APIs, and security features. The analysis highlights two major breaking changes in the browser's UI helper functions as part of a large-scale refactoring effort.

Browser & UI API

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public function chrome::GetTabbedBrowserCount(Profile* profile) has been removed from chrome/browser/ui/browser_finder.h. Callers must now iterate through ProfileBrowserCollection to count browsers of a specific type. (ebb63bf)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Public function signatures in chrome/browser/ui/singleton_tabs.h and chrome/browser/ui/chrome_pages.h have been changed. Functions such as ShowSingletonTab and GetSingletonTabNavigateParams now accept a BrowserWindowInterface* instead of a Browser*. This change is part of the ongoing 'Bedrock' project to decouple UI logic from the concrete Browser class. (7f61ab9)
  • As part of the 'Bedrock' refactoring, BrowserCollection::GetLastActiveBrowser() has been introduced as a modern replacement for chrome::FindLastActive(). This continues the effort to reduce direct dependencies on Browser and BrowserList across the codebase. (27f213d) (ed4f7e5)

UI & UX

  • Several visual and behavioral bugs have been fixed for the experimental Vertical Tabs feature, improving animations when closing pinned tabs and ensuring hovercard behavior on group headers is consistent with individual tabs. (c3f3459) (ff4276f)
  • A bug in WebUI-based browsers was fixed where the back/forward navigation buttons would not update their enabled state after an in-page navigation, only refreshing on tab changes. (6f46759)
  • The context menu icon for the 'Glic' feature now uses a colorful raster image on macOS to ensure it displays correctly in native menus. (cbe5901)

Security & Privacy

  • To improve security diagnostics, a new, more detailed metric WebAuthentication.Enclave.PinRenewalActionOutcome has been added for WebAuthn. This replaces the now-obsolete WebAuthentication.PinRenewalFailureCause histogram. (8771676)
  • As a security hardening measure, ADVANCED_MEMORY_SAFETY_CHECKS have been enabled in javascript_dialogs::TabModalDialogManager to mitigate potential memory-related vulnerabilities. (8460673)

Internals & Refactoring

  • A performance optimization was made to base::CheckedSpan by exempting thread-local storage (TLS) getters from its bounds checks, as they were identified as a performance bottleneck in profiles. (d5c3cbd)
  • Code quality in the Optimization Guide component was improved by applying clang-tidy checks, primarily replacing const std::string& with std::string_view in function parameters to reduce string conversions. (bd057d6)
  • New unit tests have been added to improve test coverage for the Tab Search page handler's behavior when split tabs are removed and for the base::span container. (dd1e9b6) (346b40a)

2026-03-21

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-21

Overview

This digest covers 45 commits, analyzing 13 relevant to browser embedders, from (7688577)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((7688577)) to (7689835)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((7689835)). Highlights include a breaking change to HDCP 2.3 reporting on Windows for PlayReady content, a new performance optimization for the WebRequest API, and several UI and security enhancements.

Media & DRM

  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Windows, the Encrypted Media Extensions (EME) API will now report HDCP 2.3 as unsupported for PlayReady-protected content. Previously, queries for HDCP 2.2 and 2.3 were treated identically. This change corrects the capability reporting, as the underlying Windows OS does not yet differentiate between the two versions. Content providers relying on the previous behavior for HDCP 2.3 may need to adjust their logic. (87eee40)

Extensions

  • A new feature flag, kOptimizeWebRequestProxy, has been introduced to change how network requests are proxied for extensions. When enabled, it avoids globally routing all subresource requests through the browser process just because a <webview>-using extension is installed. Instead, proxying is more targeted, applying only to requests from actual <webview> frames or when an extension with webRequest or declarativeNetRequest permissions is active. This is expected to improve performance by allowing more direct renderer-to-network requests. (95c9427)
  • A new histogram has been added to track the length of text set on extension action badges (chrome.action.setBadgeText). This data will inform a future decision on whether to impose a length limit to prevent performance issues from excessively long badge strings. (73e8489)
  • Internal refactoring continues to decouple extension-related code from the main //chrome module. Several constants and preference keys related to the Chrome Web Store (kAppsGalleryDownloadURL, kCWSInfoTimestamp) have been moved into the //extensions component. (1320e33) (83b14c7)

Security & Networking

  • As part of ongoing security hardening, validation for the Origin header on navigation requests has been tightened. Logging for unexpected Origin headers is now restricted to renderer-initiated navigations, fixing an issue where browser-initiated navigations (like going 'Back' to a POST redirect) were incorrectly flagged. (bbbb03f)
  • The internal ServiceWorker framework has been refactored to use the type-safe content::ChildProcessId wrapper instead of raw integers for representing process IDs, improving code clarity and safety. (0e161e8)
  • The internal logic for checking Certificate Transparency (CT) requirements has been refactored for efficiency. The delegate is no longer checked for policy overrides if the Signed Certificate Timestamps (SCTs) already comply with the policy. (5ade5a4)

UI & UX

  • A new context menu item, 'Ask Gemini' (or 'Summarize this page with Gemini'), has been added. This feature, part of the Glic (Gemini in Chrome) integration, is being introduced via an experiment. (8df2c9f)
  • The UI for the Saved Tab Groups "Resumption Rail" has been updated to correctly handle focus traversal, ensuring the 'more' button is focused in the correct order relative to its parent item. Additionally, the 'everything button' is now correctly hidden for new user profiles that are in a grace period for the feature's IPH promo. (ac30f15) (4ac6e41)
  • The Certificate Manager subpage (chrome://settings/certificates) has been migrated from Polymer to Lit as part of the ongoing WebUI modernization effort. (082abf4)

Infrastructure & Bug Fixes

  • A crash has been fixed in the internal ActorKeyedService framework. The crash occurred when a task completion callback tried to query the list of active tasks. The fix ensures the completed task is removed from the active list *before* callbacks are run. (24300df)

2026-03-20

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-20

Overview

This summary for the main branch on March 20, 2026, covers 431 relevant commits out of 583 total ((0939042)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((0939042)), (f08dfd5)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((f08dfd5))). The day was marked by numerous significant breaking changes affecting extensions, media handling, payments, and native event observation on Windows. A major security policy change was also landed, altering how Certificate Transparency is enforced.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING A major behavioral change to Certificate Transparency (CT) policy now causes Chromium to ignore Signed Certificate Timestamps (SCTs) delivered via OCSP responses by default. Websites relying solely on this mechanism for CT compliance may now trigger certificate trust errors. This is controlled by the CertificateTransparencyIgnoreOcspScts feature, which is now on by default. (4d1a2f7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Nine methods have been removed from the chrome.passwordsPrivate extension API, including deleteAllPasswordManagerData, showAddShortcutDialog, and isAccountStorageActive. Extensions must migrate to the new password_manager.mojom interface. (17f18ce)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Windows, the native event observer interface in base/message_loop/message_pump_win.h has been refactored. The Will/DidDispatchMSG methods are renamed to Will/DidRunNativeEvent and their signature now takes a uintptr_t identifier instead of a const MSG&. (fe915f0) (6be9b03)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The release callback for media::VideoFrame has a new signature. It now receives an additional boolean lost_resource to indicate if the underlying shared image was lost, requiring updates for all custom VideoFrame producers. (e614ea8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING IbanManager has been refactored from a profile-keyed service to a per-tab object, and IbanManagerFactory has been removed. Embedders must now retrieve the manager via PaymentsAutofillClient. (9ef7381)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The kSynchronizedScrolling feature and ScrollHandlerState enum have been removed from the compositor. This changes the public API of the scheduler by altering the signature of Scheduler::SetTreePrioritiesAndScrollState in cc/scheduler/scheduler.h. (65b5613)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The DeletePermission method overload that only takes a const url::Origin& has been removed from the public ActorLoginPermissionService interface. Callers must now use the overload that also requires a display_name. (8f184f3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The EvalJsResult::IsError() helper method has been removed from the public testing API in content/public/test/browser_test_utils.h. Tests should be updated to use EXPECT_FALSE(ExecJs(...)) or an equivalent matcher. (e812124)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The recently added google_apis::GetCdmCertificate() function has been reverted and removed. Any code that adopted this new API will be broken. (2172bc8)

APIs & Extensions

  • A new method, CreatedBySessionRestore(), has been added to the public BrowserWindowInterface, allowing components to determine if a browser window was created as part of a session restore operation. (f200ba1)
  • The experimental TabStrip API was updated to ensure tabs closed via the API are correctly recorded in the browser's history as user-initiated actions. (c4338c9)

Security & Networking

  • DNS-over-HTTPS (DoH) requests will now use an empty IsolationInfo, which allows Alt-Svc information to be persisted and enables faster adoption of QUIC on subsequent connections. (6ca275c)
  • The SecurityPrincipal::IsWebUI() method now correctly identifies chrome-search:// as a WebUI scheme, aligning its behavior with site isolation checks and potentially affecting embedders relying on the previous definition. (2ba6492)
  • The networking stack now supports creating empty IsolationInfo and related key objects with a non-default partition. This allows non-web requests to be partitioned from regular requests without needing to be transient. (45a3637)

Browser UI & Features

  • The download bubble has been enhanced to display a "Checking for safety..." scanning animation while waiting for Safe Browsing content checks to complete, improving user feedback. (2cd8d6e)
  • A bug was fixed to ensure that infobars are correctly shown when a browser session is restored asynchronously. (dd30ff1)

2026-03-19

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-19

Overview

This summary covers 370 relevant commits out of 514 total for the main branch on March 19, 2026. The day was marked by a large number of breaking changes across core APIs, browser features, and developer tools, requiring significant attention from embedders. Key updates include modifications to content::WebContents, the removal of the Tab Organization service, and an upgrade of the core ICU library. The first relevant commit was (37f1b6e) and the last was (b26b738).

Core APIs & Interfaces

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The content::WebContents interface has a new pure virtual method, RequestSelectionBounds. All embedders subclassing content::WebContents must implement this new method to avoid build failures. (8020df7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The core International Components for Unicode (ICU) library has been upgraded to version 78.2. This can cause significant behavioral changes in internationalization APIs, such as URL formatting and IDNA spoof checking, potentially breaking downstream parsers that expect older formats (e.g., timezone formatting changing from "GMT" to "GMT+0"). (f72d48e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The content::ContentBrowserClient interface has a new virtual method, ShouldDenySharedWorkerExtendedLifetime. While it has a default implementation, embedders may need to override it to correctly handle the new SharedWorkerExtendedLifetimeEnabled enterprise policy. (d53e73e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The signature for BrowserUserEducationInterface::CanShowFeaturePromo() has been changed to include a PassKey parameter, breaking all existing calls to this public method. (48c0c42)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The public method WebView::SetCrashedOverlayView in ui/views/controls/webview/webview.h has been re-introduced after a recent removal. Code that was updated to accommodate its removal will now be broken. (ccf079f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: Two public methods, CanCopySharedImageToGLTextureViaSkia and CanCopySharedImageToGLTextureViaTextureCopy, have been removed from the GLES2Interface. Embedders directly calling these low-level GPU interface methods will need to find alternative implementations. (eeb6937) (5a7ae68)

Browser UI & Features

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The Tab Organization feature has been removed. This includes the TabOrganizationService, its factory, and the associated IDC_ORGANIZE_TABS command. Projects that integrated with this service will break and must remove their dependencies. (4a44e72)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The API for showing context menus from WebUI toolbar buttons has been updated. The showContextMenu method in toolbar_ui_api.mojom now requires a gfx.mojom.Rect (screen_rect) instead of a gfx.mojom.Point. WebUI implementations must be updated to pass the full bounding rectangle of the anchor element. (4c3a21b)
  • On macOS, the First Run dialog has been fully migrated from a native Cocoa implementation to the cross-platform Views framework. The public-facing ShowFirstRunDialog() function remains, but its implementation is now exclusively Views-based. (5ce0c2b)

Web APIs

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The error handling of the Digital Credentials API has been changed. When a user cancels a request, the API now rejects the promise with a NotAllowedError instead of a NetworkError. Client-side code must be updated to catch the new error type for proper handling of user cancellation. (f6d8d2e)

Networking & Security

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The signature for StoragePartitionImpl::RevokeNetworkForNoncesInNetworkContext and its underlying Mojo interface has been changed. Callers must now pass a network::ConnectionAllowlists struct instead of a set of strings, breaking existing integrations. (996bdae)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: Several expired InsecureDownloadSecurityStatus enum values have been removed from the public header components/download/public/common/download_stats.h. Any code referencing these enums will no longer compile. (7fa5463)
  • Security has been enhanced by adding validation for headers provided by renderer-initiated navigations. This helps prevent potential exploits from a compromised renderer process. (fdab935)
  • Performance of HTTP/2 has been improved by proactively creating a SpdySession on preconnect, and WebSocket throughput is enhanced by increasing the deflate stream chunk size from 4KB to 32KB. (f9e7758) (e0f9987)

Developer Tools

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The DevTools DOM protocol has been updated. The isAdRelated boolean property on nodes is now replaced by a more detailed adProvenance object. Tools that inspect the DOM for ad-related information must be updated to parse this new structure. (f9c4f88)
  • The WebAuthn domain in the Chrome DevTools Protocol has been updated to support hmac-secret and hmac-secret-mc options for virtual authenticators, improving testing capabilities. (012c80d)

Autofill & Extensions

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: The AutofillManager::Observer interface has been modified. The signature for OnSuggestionsHidden now includes a SuggestionHidingReason, which will break existing observer implementations. (95237ac)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING CHANGE: Several unused ServerFieldType enum values have been removed from components/autofill/core/browser/field_types.h and the autofill_private extensions API. Code referencing these types will no longer compile. (73deb77)
  • The chrome://policy page has been updated to display the status of extension install policies, providing more transparency for administrators. (49588c7)

Build & Test Infrastructure

  • On Windows, installer crash reports will now include logs from the updater, providing more context for diagnosing installation failures. (a8f6993)
  • A common MockSessionSyncService has been introduced to reduce code duplication and simplify the creation of sync-related unit tests. (ae7c922)
  • The base library has been updated to encourage the use of try_emplace for more efficient map insertions, preventing unnecessary object construction in many cases. (ca804d1)

2026-03-18

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-18

Overview

This digest covers 752 commits from the main branch on March 18, 2026, with 577 identified as relevant to developers working on Chromium-based projects. The changes span from commit (d723889) to (8758f77). This summary highlights significant API changes, security enhancements, and updates to core browser components, with a special focus on breaking changes that require developer action.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public content::SecurityPrincipal interface has a new pure virtual method, IsWebUI(). Embedders with custom implementations must add an override for this method to avoid build failures. (b7f87db)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The return type of AttributeType::field_type() in the Autofill component has been changed from FieldType to std::optional<FieldType>. This signature change also increments the WebDatabase version, making new databases incompatible with older clients. (d2a8a16)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public function chrome::FindBrowserWithWindow() now returns a BrowserWindowInterface* instead of a Browser*. Callers must be updated to handle the new interface type. (4b4f769)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The virtual method RequestPermissions has been removed from the public content::PermissionControllerDelegate interface. Embedders must migrate to using RequestPermissionsFromCurrentDocument. (4035ccf)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public methods IsFullyPopulated() on net::NetworkIsolationKey and net::NetworkAnonymizationKey have been removed and replaced with IsEmpty(). This requires code updates at all call sites. (77bcc0d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The VisibilityTimerTabHelper class has been moved from chrome/browser to a new component at components/visibility_timer, breaking existing include paths. (a34795a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method PrerenderHandle::GetHandleId() has been removed. The prerender API now exclusively uses PrerenderHostId as its identifier. (df805b8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The virtual method FindFirstPartySetEntries has been removed from the public net::CookieAccessDelegate class. Custom implementations of this delegate will no longer compile and must be updated. (62b2995)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method pin_infobar_controller() has been removed from the BrowserWindowFeatures class. Embedders should now use the static method PinInfoBarController::From(BrowserWindowInterface*). (6d4a020)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A recent change to make media::VideoCaptureObserver inherit from base::CheckedObserver has been reverted. This constitutes a signature change to the public media API. (de612ca)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public constant kDefaultMaxSocketsPerProxyChain has been removed from net/socket/client_socket_pool_manager.h. Code referencing this constant for socket pool configuration will no longer compile. (db73207)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING IDNA 2008 ContextJ rules for URL processing are now enabled by default. This is a significant behavioral change to URL canonicalization that may affect how internationalized domain names are parsed and handled. (3ae9984)

Browser & UI

  • A new method, DetachWebContents(tabs::TabHandle tab), has been added to the TabListInterface. This allows for detaching a tab's WebContents for use elsewhere, transferring ownership to the caller. (81e7070)
  • Significant refactoring has been done to support a future WebUI-based pinned toolbar and omnibox popup, including new interfaces on the LocationBar and abstractions for toolbar action containers. (9d0cfb3) (74cd6a5) (c9f6839)
  • The floating GlicWidget now supports native window frames (TYPE_WINDOW) on all platforms, including Windows, when the kGlicUseNonClient feature is enabled. This allows the OS to handle window decorations and management. (25104b6)
  • A bug in the "Tabs from other devices" feature has been fixed where middle-clicking a synced tab now correctly restores its history instead of just opening a new tab. (0c9b0e3)
  • A number of obsolete or fully-enabled feature flags have been removed, including media::kLiveTranslate, kShortcutsNotApps, kPushMessagingBackgroundMode, and device::features::kOpenXrExtendedFeatureSupport. (0f8d479) (3151653) (756982f)
  • A new feature flag, ViewsClickjackingProtection, has been added to chrome://flags to facilitate testing of upcoming clickjacking mitigations in the Views framework. (369de3d)

Networking & Security

  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in DevTools cookie handlers (e.g., Network.clearBrowserCookies) has been fixed. The issue was resolved by using a base::WeakPtr in an async callback filter. (e4fa6ce)
  • A filename spoofing vulnerability in the Windows "Save As" dialog has been fixed. The download target determiner now iteratively sanitizes filenames to expose hidden .url extensions. (9c22990)
  • The network service's UDP socket layer now supports Source-Specific Multicast (SSM). The JoinGroup and LeaveGroup Mojo methods now accept an optional source_address parameter. (7cfd086)
  • To prevent spoofing, the "settings overridden" dialog for search engines will now display the search engine's domain URL instead of its short name for non-prepopulated providers. (5a827af)
  • Preparatory work for encrypting session files has landed, including adding an optional Encryptor parameter to CommandStorageBackend and making FileOpeningJob ref-counted. (323cc8f) (c0bb274) (2761bb9)
  • Third-party dependencies for sqlite (to v3.51.3) and expat have been rolled, incorporating several upstream bug fixes and security patches. (016a5e5) (703b76e)

Extensions

  • The extension updater now includes "session hunting" logic to retry failed downloads from the Chrome Web Store. It increments the authuser query parameter to resolve authentication issues with multiple signed-in Google accounts. (56bfb92)
  • A dangling pointer crash in BackgroundContentsService has been fixed. The fix uses a WeakPtr to the profile in a delayed task callback to prevent a crash when the profile is destroyed. (9a8f65c)
  • Extension telemetry has been enhanced to extract URLs, file paths, and script code from scripting.executeScript activity logs, improving visibility into extension behavior. (c789fa5)
  • A bug in SimpleFeature::IsCommandLineSwitchEnabled has been fixed to correctly parse switch values (e.g., --switch-name=1) instead of incorrectly checking for the combined string. (7e12bbd)

Autofill

  • The OmniboxAutofillDelegate is now created for ChromePaymentsAutofillClient, which extends omnibox payment suggestion capabilities. (f68dec5)
  • A bug causing redundant separators in Autofill address suggestions for profiles with partial information has been resolved. (da6798d)
  • Autofill suggestions triggered from the accessibility annotator will now be anchored to the text caret (kCaret) when possible, improving the positioning of the suggestion popup. (73accab)

Base, Developer Tools & Testing

  • base::ScopedObservation has been updated with a new template parameter to prepare for future dangling pointer detection. The change is source-compatible for now but signals a future modification. (940f2c7) (adc3bcf)
  • The DevTools timeline recorder will now capture up to 1800 screenshots (up from 450) at a reduced resolution (250x250), improving temporal detail without increasing the memory footprint. (bac2172)
  • The chrome://version page now displays a "Variations Source" field, indicating whether feature variations are loaded from a field trial, the Finch server, a local file, or the command line. (2266327)
  • The unused MockBaseWindow class and its related files have been removed from ui/base/test. (b809056)

2026-03-17

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-17

Overview

This summary covers 526 relevant commits out of 683 total from the main branch, focusing on changes that impact developers working on Chromium-based projects. The day saw an unusually high number of breaking changes across core APIs, including networking, GPU, sync, and security. Key improvements were also made to extension infrastructure, security features, and testing utilities. The first relevant commit was (3233143) and the last was (e202271).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public crypto/kdf.h API has been renamed. DeriveKeyPbkdf2HmacSha1 is now Pbkdf2HmacSha1, and DeriveKeyScrypt is now Scrypt. Direct usage of the old names will fail to compile. (640d483)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The syncer::DataTypeSyncBridge interface has a new pure virtual method, TrimAllSupportedFieldsFromRemoteSpecifics. All custom sync bridge implementations must be updated to provide this method. (a531d36)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public media::AudioDecoderConfig class has removed its legacy constructor and Initialize method. Embedders must now use the versions that take a ChannelLayoutConfig object. (c9acbb5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The HttpNetworkSession::SocketPoolType enum has been converted to a stricter enum class. Callers must update to use scoped enum values like HttpNetworkSession::SocketPoolType::kNormal. (6738f12)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ExtensionSystem::GetDependentExtensions method has been removed. Code must now retrieve the SharedModuleService via its factory to access this functionality. (7f8a972)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The process_id field in the public ServiceWorkerVersionInfo struct has been changed from int to the strongly-typed content::ChildProcessId, requiring code updates. (6e9eb7a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the net::URLRequest and services::network::ResourceRequest APIs, the field allows_device_bound_session_registration has been renamed to allows_device_bound_sessions. (3e700ff)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The gpu::gles2::GLES2Interface::CopySharedImageToGLTextureViaTextureCopy method signature has changed, removing the src_size parameter. (d713cdd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method WebURLRequest::SetHttpOriginIfNeeded has been removed from third_party/blink/public/platform/web_url_request.h, breaking any external callers. (4852d79)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The blink::mojom::StorageArea mojom interface has changed. Methods like Put and Delete now require a structured StorageAreaSourcePtr instead of a simple string for the source parameter. (e431bee)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The PrivacySandboxService interface has been cleaned up, removing the public methods GetRequiredPromptType and PromptActionOccurred. (a7c87e2)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Deprecated Add(int, ...) and Remove(int) methods have been removed from ChildProcessSecurityPolicyImpl. Callers must use the versions that accept the strongly-typed ChildProcessId. (62ce02f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The BrowserUserEducationInterface::MaybeShowFeaturePromo method now returns a bool instead of void. Implementations and mocks of this interface must be updated. (9430812)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several obsolete command-line switches have been removed, including kCrashOnHangThreads, kEnableCloudPrintProxy, and kNoServiceAutorun. (08ad5f9)

Security & Privacy

  • The crypto library has been enhanced with support for the X25519 key-exchange algorithm, adding new types and functions to crypto/keypair and crypto/kex. (e5ebc0b)
  • The sct-auditing feature for Signed Certificate Timestamp auditing is now permanently enabled in Chrome-branded desktop builds and the feature flag has been removed. (24258bf)
  • Password store errors are now propagated to the WebUI event router, allowing for better UI feedback when the OS-level credential store is locked. (9346836)
  • Several base:: string utility functions like TrimString have been annotated with LIFETIME_BOUND, which may introduce new compile-time warnings or errors for code passing temporary strings, preventing use-after-free bugs. (95bfd23)
  • The macOS GPU sandbox has been updated to tighten restrictions. (7082f06)

Networking

  • A new static method, HttpNoVarySearchData::ParseFromHeaderValue(), allows for more efficient parsing of the No-Vary-Search header directly from its string value. (cecaa7e)
  • The signature of NetworkAnonymizationKey helper methods has been changed to take SchemefulSite by value (std::move), which may cause build failures but improves performance by reducing copies. (d3c3966)
  • External protocol handling logic has been modularized into its own component at //chrome/browser/external_protocol to improve dependency hygiene. (2542f99)

APIs & Core Internals

  • A race condition in ProfileManager that could cause crashes during rapid profile closure and reopening has been fixed with a new synchronization mechanism. (052f564)
  • Autofill's country code validation, IsValidCountryCode, has been strengthened to check against a compile-time set of known valid codes instead of just format. (f09a045)
  • A new command-line flag, --forced-translate-language, has been added to allow developers to force the translation prompt for any language, ignoring 'never translate' preferences. (2519ad1)
  • A significant code migration was completed to replace raw Browser* pointers with the more abstract BrowserWindowInterface* in many areas, preparing for future architectural changes. (84b2352)

Extensions

  • A new ExtensionInstallTimePermissionProvider has been introduced to allow the Site Settings page to correctly reflect permissions (e.g., geolocation) granted to an extension via its manifest. (7307848)
  • Performance during shutdown has been improved by optimizing the removal of extension event listeners, avoiding a costly O(N^2) operation for extensions with many filtered listeners. (dec6118)
  • For enterprise reporting, extension installation, update, and uninstallation events will now include the profile's user name and identifier for better administrative context. (d7a2a43)

GPU

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The shared_image_swap_chain capability in gpu::SharedImageCapabilities is now restricted to Windows only. This removes a public API field for other platforms like macOS, which will break embedders checking for this capability. (b800237)

UI & Features

  • The core UI interface ui::BaseWindow has a new virtual method, bool CanResize(ui::WindowResizeFailureReason& reason), allowing callers to check if a window can be resized and why not. (6f8a29a)
  • The 'What's New' page is now enabled by default. (602e50c)
  • Strings in the NSS PKCS #11 dialog have been updated to use "PIN" instead of "password" and "Unlock" instead of "Sign in", clarifying the action for hardware security key users. (4fb5942)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • The DevTools protocol was refactored to consolidate ad-related ancestry types. Page.AdScriptAncestry and Audits.AdAncestry have been removed in favor of a new, unified Network.AdAncestry type. (1b98f07)
  • A new test-only override was added to AccountsPolicyManager to skip the profile deletion confirmation dialog, unblocking automated testing of profile deletion policies. (1da9186)
  • The embedded fake sync server can now simulate DeviceInfo deletion by generating tombstones when it receives a SyncDisabledEvent, improving integration tests. (84f08ba)

Feature & Flag Removals

  • The obsolete and unused feature flag kEnableServiceWorkersForChromeScheme has been removed from the codebase. (7377a7f)
  • The unlaunched feature flag autofill-unmask-card-request-timeout and its related code were removed. (313533c)
  • The WebAuthn experiment WebAuthnImmediateGetAutoselect and its associated logic have been removed. (fc839e4)

2026-03-16

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-16

Overview

This summary covers 405 relevant commits out of 548 total for the main branch on March 16, 2026. The day saw a significant number of breaking changes across core UI, identity, and serialization APIs, requiring action from embedders. Key updates also include the enablement of Device Bound Session Credentials by default, critical fixes to Windows registry handling, and continued refactoring of the browser's core interfaces. The first commit was (4e4b30e) and the last was (44f0d80).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The widely used helper function chrome::FindTabbedBrowser and related functions like FindBrowserWithProfile have changed their return type from Browser* to the more abstract BrowserWindowInterface*. This is part of the "Bedrock" project to reduce dependencies on the concrete Browser class. Embedders must update their code to use the new interface. (4a045d1) (ba815fd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The IdentityManager initialization flow has been simplified by removing the now-redundant account_consistency parameter from its builder and the MutableProfileOAuth2TokenServiceDelegate constructor. Embedders building or initializing IdentityManager must update their calls. (63307d4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the core base library, the serialization method base::PickleIterator::ReadStringPiece16 has been removed due to potential unaligned memory access issues. Code using this for deserialization must be updated to use ReadString16 or an alternative. (9ba8054)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method WebView::SetCrashedOverlayView has been removed. Embedders must migrate to TakeCrashedOverlayView to provide a custom view when a WebContents crashes, which now correctly handles view ownership. (cbbb542)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The TabStrip Mojo API's OnDataChanged event has been restructured from a struct to a union, providing more specific details on tab, group, or split-tab changes. This is a breaking change for any client using this API. (e2acfde) (d41b221)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ServiceWorkerState::Observer::OnWorkerStop method signature now includes a blink::ServiceWorkerToken. This is critical for correctly tracking service worker lifecycles and requires embedders observing extension service workers to update their observer implementations. (2cc7179)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core UI method PlatformClipboard::IsSelectionOwner in ui/ozone/public/platform_clipboard.h has been changed from returning a synchronous bool to an asynchronous callback-based signature, requiring updates for all callers. (f56f565)

API & Extensions

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ServiceWorkerContextCoreObserver interface methods now use the strongly-typed content::ChildProcessId instead of a raw int. Any class implementing this observer must update its method signatures. (491060e) (1f8433c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the Autofill component, the public method FormFieldData::selected_option() has been removed. Code must be updated to use selected_option_text() instead. (2348edd)
  • A new feature, guarded by the kPasswordStorePropagatesActionableErrors flag, allows ActionableErrors (e.g., 'trusted vault unlock required') to be propagated from the password store backend to the UI layer. (85a01d2)
  • A race condition in the Storage Access API was fixed where permission overrides could fail. The renderer-to-browser IPC for this has been removed in favor of the browser synchronously updating its state before notifying the renderer. (3fe82e5)

Browser UI & Features

  • A new method, HasPromoBeenDismissed, was added to the BrowserUserEducationInterface to allow querying whether a promotional message has been dismissed by the user. (55fc22d)
  • The anchoring logic for chooser bubbles, such as the USB device chooser, has been improved to correctly position the bubble when the browser is in fullscreen mode on macOS. (c9c1494) (4283b38)
  • A crash when reverting certain tab drags involving tab groups has been fixed. (75c313d)
  • A bug was fixed where a keyboard override for proceeding through a security interstitial would cause a crash on the "man-in-the-middle" software warning page, which has no "proceed" option. (583dd04)

Security & Identity

  • Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) has been enabled by default, and its associated origin trials have been removed. This changes the default behavior for session registration to enhance security against cookie theft. (ebf37aa)
  • Stricter IPC validation has been added for WindowOpenDisposition values sent from a compromised renderer. The browser now verifies that the disposition is a value a well-behaved renderer could have sent, hardening against renderer exploits. (4ccc2d9)
  • Logic was added to connect a new user preference to the dynamic process isolation state, allowing it to be set early during startup via registry keys on Windows. This lays the groundwork for future UI and policy controls over process isolation. (605f43d)

Base & Core Utilities

  • A critical bug has been fixed in Windows registry handling where handles to predefined keys (e.g., HKEY_CURRENT_USER) were being improperly closed. This prevents potential breakage of registry overrides set via command line. (b2a5c41)
  • The base::i18n time formatting logic for HTTP dates was updated to use a literal 'GMT' string. This ensures compliance with RFC 7231, as an upcoming ICU release would otherwise change the output to 'GMT+0', potentially breaking HTTP interactions. (81cd430)
  • A new PRESUBMIT.py check has been added to //chrome to disallow new usages of base::RunLoop. This encourages developers to use better patterns that avoid common re-entrancy bugs. (d44615c)
  • RenderProcessHost now records and exposes the process launch time, allowing for more detailed startup performance analysis. (8d81f78)

Networking

  • The Public Suffix List (PSL) has been updated to the latest version (revision 3c5eb8e). (a4dd319)
  • The SPDY protocol implementation will now track the time requests spend in the pending queue when the session is at its concurrent stream limit, providing new metrics for performance analysis. (4c67600)
  • For Protected Audience auctions, the kProtectedAudienceCorsSafelistKVv2Signals feature flag was removed. CORS preflights are now enforced for web-initiated ad-auction-trusted-signals-request content types, improving security. (635cc50)

GPU

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The functions CanCopySIDirectlyToGLTexture() and CanCopySIToGLTextureViaSkia() have been moved into the GLES2Interface. This is a breaking change to a core graphics API that affects embedders with custom video-to-texture rendering paths. (fe5ea1e) (2a7f0ca)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • New test support classes, FakeDomStorageDatabase and ScopedDomStorageDatabaseFactoryForTesting, were added to enable comprehensive testing of all database recovery outcomes for DOM Storage without modifying production code. (b6c7455)
  • The logic for retrieving the application locale in MetricsServiceClient was consolidated to match the logic used by the Variations service (Finch). This prevents data skew where UMA might log a different locale than the one used for field trial assignments. (ed4ddec)
  • The primary Chromium fuzzing README.md was rewritten for clarity, improving the developer experience for security researchers and engineers working on fuzz testing. (ab075ea)

2026-03-15

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-15

Overview

This digest covers 8 commits, with a focus on changes relevant to embedders on Windows and macOS. One major breaking change was identified in the base macOS utilities, where a public enum for system settings was modified. Other updates include UI improvements for Vertical Tabs and internal testing enhancements.

Browser APIs (macOS)

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public base::mac::SystemSettingsPane enum, used with OpenSystemSettingsPane to open macOS System Settings, has been updated. Several enum values were removed and replaced to align with upcoming macOS versions. For example, kDateTime is now kGeneral_DateTime, and kPrivacySecurity_Pasteboard is now kPrivacySecurity_PasteFromOtherApps. Embedders on macOS using the old enum values will encounter build failures. (6c38bdc)

UI & UX

  • The Vertical Tabs feature received animation improvements to prevent visual jank. The changes ensure that animating tabs are correctly scrolled into view, especially in overflow scenarios, and that the new tab button remains flush with the scroll view during animations. (f303bc0) (471bde2)

Testing

  • The TabSearchPageHandler unit tests were refactored to reset the handler during teardown, preventing spurious notifications that could mask test expectations. Separately, a dangling pointer was fixed in OptOutBlocklistTest to resolve a memory safety issue. (b75ca23) (60e4de8)

2026-03-14

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-14

Overview

This digest covers 75 commits from the Chromium repository. After filtering for changes relevant to Windows and macOS embedders, focusing on browser APIs, UI, extensions, and security, 46 commits were analyzed. This summary highlights two major breaking changes in the UI and search components, alongside updates to security features, extension tooling, and internal refactoring. The changes span from (b1feb72) to (ea9f934).

Browser UI & Views

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The virtual method OnWidgetClosing has been removed from the public class WebUIBubbleDialogView. Embedders subclassing this view and overriding this method will experience build failures. The logic has been moved to a RegisterWindowClosingCallback pattern. (d18477d)
  • A new enum and helper class have been added to TabStripModel to allow for disabling close animations, specifically for tabs closed from the new 'Contextual tasks' feature. (592f4f6)
  • The chrome::CanSavePage() function has been refactored to operate over the BrowserWindowInterface, reducing dependencies in the Sharing Hub component. (3e8881c)
  • Miscellaneous UI and behavior fixes, including re-enabling tab movement shortcuts on macOS, adjusting the accessibility role of the Cast dialog's info icon, and various tweaks to the New Tab Page and vertical tabs. (d8e69d7) (773ba66) (0492460) (e9738c6) (6409484) (b4f6d75)

Browser Internals & Services

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: To improve consistency, methods for confirming search engine removal have been renamed. TemplateURL::RequiresDeletionConfirmation is now RequiresRemovalConfirmation, and KeywordEditorController::ShouldConfirmDeletion is now ShouldConfirmRemoval. Embedders using these public APIs for search engine management must update their code. (ea9f934)
  • The PageContentAnnotationsService API has been extended to allow observation of on-device category classifier results, an internal enhancement for content analysis. (adbe66e)
  • Internal refactoring of the CriticalUserJourneyService to align with C++ style guidelines by replacing a unique_ptr with a direct object member. (3be785e)

Security

  • The garbage collection logic for Device-Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) has been moved to SessionStore to prevent accidental deletion of valid keys. The RestoreSessionBindingKey method signature was simplified by removing a priority parameter as part of this refactor for the experimental feature. (01c58d5)
  • Logic for the experimental FedCM API has been updated to allow navigation interception when there is an embedder login request, even if general interception is not enabled. (e1dfa92)
  • The migration to Enhanced Safe Browsing (ESB) will now be disabled if the Safe Browsing or JavaScript Optimizer preferences are managed by enterprise policy. (bfa6fc4)

Extensions

  • The extension API schema compiler now supports the nodoc attribute on individual enum values, allowing certain values to be excluded from generated documentation. This affects tooling and not runtime behavior. (f698dba)
  • A series of commits performed JavaScript modernization in various extension API tests, such as downloads, fileapi, and content_settings, replacing var with let/const and updating code style. These changes do not affect production APIs. (f8bc9ad) (928ae0c) (261f692) (5614bae) (c67313e) (01a06e8) (b091a6d) (05b9e19) (b1feb72) (6fb43a7)

PDF

  • Several internal refactorings and bug fixes were made to the experimental PDF Ink Signatures feature, including simplifying stroke ID recycling, fixing a command reference bug, and updating ID types. The feature flag's expiration was also extended. (3ad31ba) (54539cd) (396a915) (f2e85a3) (f38ba5e)

Infrastructure & Tooling

  • A new ESLint check has been added to disallow the use of chrome.send() in new Desktop WebUI code, enforcing the modern Mojo-based communication pattern. (d9c984b)
  • Significant refactoring of BUILD.gn files was undertaken to modularize chrome/browser/webapps and other components, improving build structure and dependency management. (c26595f) (f68a6c0) (ee1115b)

2026-03-13

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-13

Overview

A busy day on the main branch with 606 commits, of which 437 were analyzed for developer impact. The day saw a significant number of breaking changes, particularly in the UI clipboard and core service APIs, requiring attention from embedders. Changes span from commit (1c93014) to (9bf8133).

Breaking Changes: Core APIs & Services

  • ⚠️ BREAKING As part of the Third-Party Cookie Deprecation (3PCD) cleanup, the SetTrackingProtectionEnabledFor3pcd method and tracking_protection_enabled_for_3pcd parameter have been removed from the public network::mojom::CookieManager interface. Embedders configuring the network service via this mojom will have a build break. (9bf8133)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public content::ServiceWorkerContextObserverSynchronous interface signature has been modified. The OnStoppingSync and OnStoppedSync methods now include a blink::ServiceWorkerToken parameter to provide more specific worker instance information, requiring embedders to update their observer implementations. (aa8f4cd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ResolveTokenRequest method in the federated_auth_request.mojom interface and the corresponding IdentityRegistryDelegate::OnResolve method have been refactored. Parameters are now grouped into a union, breaking existing implementations of the FedCM API. (1be409c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The 'User Notes' feature has been removed. This includes the removal of the AnnotationType.kUserNote enum value from the public third_party/blink/public/mojom/annotation/annotation.mojom interface, which will break any client code referencing this annotation type. (27f8c22)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ExtensionInstallPolicyService::CanInstallExtension method signature has changed. It now returns a std::optional<std::u16string> to provide a localized message explaining why an extension is blocked, instead of a simple boolean. Integrations that check for extension installation permissions must be updated. (f53de3f)

Breaking Changes: UI & Platform Clipboard

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core clipboard API has been significantly refactored to be more asynchronous. The synchronous ui::Clipboard::IsFormatAvailable method has been removed entirely and replaced by the asynchronous GetAllAvailableFormats. This requires callers to adapt to a callback-based pattern. (54777b8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ui::PlatformClipboard::IsSelectionOwner method signature has been reverted to its synchronous form (bool IsSelectionOwner(...)) after a brief period as an asynchronous method. Any code that adapted to the recent asynchronous version will break and must be reverted. (f80ba53) (8dc8619)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In the Ozone platform interface, PlatformClipboard::OfferClipboardData has been made fully asynchronous by removing its synchronous OfferDataClosure callback. This breaks the API contract for clients implementing or using this part of the Ozone platform. (9dc355d)

APIs, Extensions, and Security

  • A major internal refactoring of the Tab Strip Service API was completed to make it platform-agnostic. While this is a large architectural change involving new adapters and interfaces, it is designed to be internal and should not break embedders using the established public interfaces. (c9b4d76)
  • The PDFiumEngineClient interface for the PDF viewer was modernized to use base::span for buffer handling in its SubmitForm and InstallBuffer methods, improving type safety for embedders. (0299cee)
  • On Windows, Google Credential Provider (GCPW) sign-in flows now support security keys. The GCPW process acts as a broker to access the security key device, improving 2-Step Verification support in enterprise environments. (b85ad72)
  • The CopySharedImageToGLTextureViaTextureCopy method has been moved from an internal implementation to the public gpu::gles2::GLES2Interface, making it available for embedders to use directly for GPU operations. (6482daf)

Browser UI & Features

  • The feature flag for loyalty card filling in Autofill (kAutofillEnableLoyaltyCardsFilling) has been removed, enabling the feature by default for all users. (9d45f2f)
  • On macOS, window restoration behavior has been improved to always restore windows to their correct 'spaces' (virtual desktops), even when the application is manually quit and restarted. (f9a6ce0)
  • The First-Run Experience (FRE) sign-in page now includes a 'Don't sign in' button on the native toolbar, providing a clearer path for users who wish to skip sign-in. (ee4d985)

Build & Infrastructure

  • Work continues on improving dependency hygiene by modularizing several browser components into their own build targets, including //chrome/browser/memory, //chrome/browser/unified_consent, and //chrome/browser/ui/frame. (15e29f9) (ae443f5) (91da409)

2026-03-12

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-12

Overview

This summary covers 489 relevant commits out of a total of 692 on the main branch for March 12, 2026. The changes span from commit (156499f) to (115d9a2). This digest highlights multiple breaking changes to core networking, UI, and service APIs, alongside significant behavioral changes in extension messaging and the Viz display service. Developers building on Chromium should review the breaking changes section carefully.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The return type for HttpStream::GetTotalReceivedBytes() and GetTotalSentBytes() has been changed from int64_t to net::ByteSize. This API signature change in net/http/http_stream.h will cause build failures for any code calling these methods. (5895f46)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature for net::ClientSocketPool::PreconnectCompletionCallback has changed to include a std::unique_ptr<ClientSocketHandle> as its second argument. Embedders using this networking callback must update their implementations. (88f44d9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A revert has removed the MutableNetworkTrafficAnnotationTag parameter from several core networking functions, including ClientSocketPool::RequestSocket and ClientSocketHandle::Init. This is a signature change to stable, public networking APIs that will break builds. (5ca41b4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The macOS-specific NativeFrameViewMacClient class has been removed. Embedders on macOS must migrate from this class to the new generic non_client_hit_test_callback_ on views::FrameView to handle draggable regions in frameless windows. (5a2ee1d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Extension messaging will now throw an error when a SharedArrayBuffer is included in a message, where it previously failed silently. This is a significant behavioral change that will break extensions relying on the old behavior. (7c8c8fb)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API for getting the bookmark clipboard format, bookmarks::BookmarkNodeData::GetBookmarkFormatType(), has been removed. Consumers must now use ui::ClipboardFormatType::BookmarkEntriesType() for clipboard and drag-and-drop operations. (dfec277)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The type field in the public sessions::tab_restore::Window struct has been renamed to window_type. Code accessing this field from the tab restore service will fail to compile. (4b2975c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The instance_ids member has been removed from the public apps::LaunchResult struct. Code inspecting this struct after an app launch will need to be updated. (42535d5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The Viz display service's LayerContextImpl::DoUpdateDisplayTree method no longer returns an error for invalid browser controls parameters (e.g., negative heights). It now silently clamps the values. Embedders relying on the error for state validation will experience a behavioral break. (d22dd57)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several constants have been removed from public headers, which will cause build failures if referenced. This includes the kSyncBookmarksBatchUploadSelectedItems switch, the kWebPermissionAPI feature flag, and the kNewFileSystemInfoCollector and kNewWinSignalsCollector enterprise feature flags. (bc0f0d2) (b62aea4) (83b1fa8)

Networking & Security

  • The ServiceWorkerAutoPreload feature is now enabled by default. This performance optimization may alter resource loading behavior for web applications that rely on Service Workers. (d500868)
  • Security has been hardened by adding a browser-side check to enforce the allow-modals sandbox attribute. This prevents a compromised renderer from showing alert(), confirm(), or prompt() dialogs if the sandboxed frame lacks the permission. (7e25766)
  • A new helper function, net::IPAddress::IsMulticast(), has been added to the networking library to check for multicast IP ranges. (5749767)
  • The network stack now includes a MIME type mapping for markdown (.md) files to text/markdown. (ade6ca0)
  • A race condition in TcpConnectJob has been fixed, improving networking stability by blocking progress while the job may be pending asynchronous destruction. (a1d8d86)
  • Policies related to "Chrome Frame," a technology deprecated for over a decade, have been completely removed from the codebase. (0fa4bed)

APIs & Extensions

  • A new media pipeline error code, PIPELINE_ERROR_HARDWARE_CONTEXT_RESET, has been introduced. This provides more specific feedback for unrecoverable hardware-related playback failures when a hardware context reset fails to recover. (bbfb0b5)
  • The PageActionObserver interface has been extended with new methods (OnAnchoredMessageShown, OnAnchoredMessageHidden) to allow observation of anchored message visibility changes. (5d8fda4)
  • The browser is now more resilient to messages from stale extension service workers. Instead of crashing, the browser will now drop IPC messages from outdated worker instances. (de506e6) (10acabd)
  • A new "Back-To-Ad" history intervention has been implemented behind a feature flag. When enabled, it modifies back/forward navigation to skip entries that are likely ad-related. (61ed18a)
  • The FormFieldData struct used by Autofill was updated with a new selected_option_text_ member. This allows Autofill to more accurately fill <select> elements by matching on the option's visible text in addition to its value. (95b08b3)

Browser & UI

  • The VerticalTabStripScrollBar has been refactored into a more generic views::RoundedScrollBar for broader use across the UI toolkit. (bb86127)
  • A bug on macOS that caused right-clicking to deselect all text in a text field has been fixed. (2c0f959)
  • The Side Panel's core UI class, SidePanelUIBase, was refactored to depend on abstract interfaces like BrowserWindowInterface instead of concrete classes, improving modularity. (f1b9f68) (bb785b0)
  • A potential dangling pointer in the global accelerator listener for macOS was fixed, preventing crashes when an accelerator is pressed after its listener has been destroyed. (9f60e90)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • As part of a broad modernization effort, base/logging.cc was updated to use std::span, improving type safety. Numerous unit tests were also updated to use modern utilities like base::test::TestFuture for cleaner asynchronous testing. (ae4a06c) (1693de3)
  • Ongoing code modularization efforts continued, with components like chrome/browser/site_isolation/, chrome/browser/status_icons/, and the undo manager being moved into their own build targets. (9c845fc) (c132b3d) (3401c7c)
  • To resolve build timeouts on macOS, vectorizer passes have been temporarily disabled during the Mach-O ThinLTO link step. (f4d0bed)
  • A large number of internal API tests for extensions, including permissions, declarativeNetRequest, nativeMessaging, and omnibox, were updated from Manifest V2 to Manifest V3. (be9c464) (b2f7a35) (868a121)

2026-03-11

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-11

Overview

This release includes 668 commits, with 511 identified as relevant to developers building on Chromium for Windows and macOS. The day was marked by a significant number of breaking changes affecting core APIs in networking, file I/O, UI, and identity management, requiring immediate attention from embedders. Other notable work includes security hardening with the network service sandbox now enabled by default on Windows, continued modularization of the chrome/browser directory, and the final removal of several features and their associated flags. For a full list of changes, see the log between the (878f494)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((878f494)) and the (1dd3629)" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer" class="text-sky-500 hover:text-sky-300 font-mono">((1dd3629)).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The base::File class's public method int WriteAtCurrentPos(const char* data, int size) has been made private. Code must be updated to use the base::span-based overload. (42d167b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The callback signature for the core networking functions ClientSocketPool::Preconnect and net::PreconnectSocketsForHttpRequest has changed from base::OnceCallback<void(int)> to base::OnceCallback<void(bool)>. Callbacks must be updated to accept a boolean success status instead of a net::Error code. (ad97a90)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature of the virtual method InputHandler::ScrollEnd has been modified to include a new compensated_scroll_delta parameter. Custom subclasses of InputHandler will need to be updated. (9ec5ed7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API content::ChildProcessHost::LaunchAsChildProcess and the associated CHILD_PLUGIN helper process type have been removed. Embedders using this method to launch child processes must find an alternative. (12d359c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The PrivacySandboxActivityTypesService and its factory have been removed. Any embedder code using this ProfileKeyedService will no longer compile. (44dc583)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The constructor for signin::internal::PrimaryAccountManager and the signin::IdentityTestEnvironment::BuildIdentityManagerForTests utility now require a metrics::ProfileMetricsService instance, breaking custom initialization of the identity stack. (de412c3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The factory method AutofillSuggestionController::GetOrCreate now requires an additional AutofillSuggestionTriggerSource argument. All calls to this method must be updated. (a29ad42)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API of AccessibilityAnnotationService has been changed. The AddObserver(), RemoveObserver(), and GetEntities() methods have been removed in favor of a new GetEntityDataProvider() method. (77cb6c6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The direct getter upgrade_notification_controller() has been removed from the public BrowserWindowFeatures class. Access is now through the static method UpgradeNotificationController::From(browser()). (1542adb)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The value of the public constant ui::display::kDefaultDisplayId has been changed from 0xff to -2, which can break custom display or window management logic. (1983a07)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The underlying type of the ui::KeyboardCode enum is now explicitly sized, which may break code that performs unsafe casting or makes assumptions about the enum's size. (e9e9cf3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The legacy FindBarIcon UI class has been completely removed from the location bar implementation. Embedders that customized or referenced this class must adapt to the new page action-based UI. (fd062ba)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The TabSearchContainer UI view class and its identifier kTabSearchContainerElementId have been removed from the tab strip, breaking any code that directly references them. (d43a49d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The deprecated "Click to Call" feature has been removed, including the public enum value PageActionIconType::kClickToCall. Code referencing this type will fail to compile. (47aad4d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The navigator.storage.estimate() API will now always report a fixed quota in Incognito mode to prevent privacy leaks. This is a significant behavioral change to a stable API. (c48f71d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On macOS, the "safe mode" for video capture and its corresponding feature flag kRetryGetVideoCaptureDeviceInfos have been removed. This removes a stable feature that allowed retrying device enumeration after a crash. (a252ae8)

APIs & Core Internals

  • The SidePanelRegistry has been refactored to use tab-scoped user data. A new From(TabInterface*) method is introduced to reduce dependencies on the Browser object. (2970137)
  • A new helper function, Uuid::AsInteger(), was added to base::Uuid to convert its string representation to a 128-bit integer. (54458bb)
  • The geolocation permission prompt now supports an "approximate-only" state, which may require updates for embedders with custom permission UIs. (03df9c4)

Browser UI & Features

  • A new HitTestCallback was added to ui::views::FrameView, providing a uniform hook for embedders to customize hit-testing and implement draggable regions in non-client areas. (7c1ec2a)
  • Several features were finalized and their flags removed, including EnableNtpEnterpriseShortcuts (now default), password-form-clientside-classifier, and multiple Autofill/Sync flags for loyalty cards. (b2574b0) (e484c32) (b937d39) (ba1bc87) (76c9ae2)
  • Foundational work was done to migrate the chrome://policy page to Mojo, managed by the new kPolicyPageMojoMigration feature flag. (b6222f0)
  • On macOS, a privacy issue was fixed where a user's full profile path was written to the clipboard when copying a bookmark. (f7d0665)
  • A new feature flag, kHistoryEnableWalMode, was added to experiment with enabling SQLite's Write-Ahead Logging (WAL) mode for the History database to improve performance. (79a5694)

Networking & Security

  • The network service sandbox is now enabled by default on Windows, significantly hardening the browser's security posture. (a0ad012)
  • A security vulnerability was fixed where the Authorization header was not stripped on cross-origin fetch() redirects when keepalive was used. (5fb6646)
  • For the new Built-In AI APIs, browser-side permission policy checks have been added. If a compromised frame attempts to use these APIs without permission, the renderer process will be terminated. (a3d6e7c)
  • The policy engine now supports policies that can have both machine and user scope values active simultaneously, with ChromeDataRegionSetting being the first to use this. (e60b2de)
  • Rollout is beginning for the client certificate provisioning policies: AutoSelectCertificateForUrl and ProvisionManagedClientCertificateFor*. (9bd8cfd)

Extensions

  • The migration of extension API tests from Manifest V2 to V3 continues for APIs like chrome.extension, management, and history, reinforcing the deprecation of MV2. (0b8b89d) (35e7b66) (c4f4143) (36f2f4c) (bdbefc8)
  • Initial support for the "devtools_page" manifest key was added to the core extensions platform. (5e32adf)
  • A crash in the chrome.cookies API was fixed by wrapping Mojo callbacks to ensure they are always invoked, preventing crashes if the network service disconnects during an API call. (70dd2cc)
  • A crash was fixed that could occur when an extension with a service worker was reloaded by ensuring stale context initialization messages are safely ignored. (d12bcc8)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • The ongoing modularization of chrome/browser continues, with page_info, data_sharing, engagement, security_events, manta, and performance_monitor being moved into their own build targets. (f7272c2) (b49f647) (1703de6) (d58da74) (d8243dd) (5e70d3b)
  • The Bookmarks component's unit tests were migrated from a custom helper to the standard MockBookmarkModelObserver with gMock expectations, improving test clarity. (e096b12)
  • The autotest tool was improved to automatically find and run the relevant test file when a corresponding implementation file (.cc) is changed. (bd89b93)

2026-03-10

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-10

Overview

This summary covers 509 relevant commits out of 707 total for the main branch on March 10, 2026. The day's changes include a significant number of breaking API and behavioral changes, particularly in graphics, autofill, and extensions. Key developments also include new APIs for UI components, security enhancements for networking protocols, and continued refactoring of core browser services. The first relevant commit was (d31051c) and the last was (39de2dc).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The automatic fallback to the ANGLE D3D9 backend on Windows has been removed. If D3D11 initialization fails, Chromium will now use software rasterization instead of D3D9, which can significantly impact performance on older systems. A new feature flag, AllowANGLED3D9Fallback, can be used to temporarily re-enable the old behavior. (282348e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public AutofillClient API has been changed. The method ShowAutofillAiFailureNotification(std::u16string message) is replaced by two new parameter-less methods: ShowAutofillAiSaveToWalletFailureNotification() and ShowAutofillAiFetchFromWalletFailureNotification(). Embedders must update their AutofillClient implementations. (b1627f2)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public methods set_storage_partition() on ServiceWorkerContextWrapper and ServiceWorkerProcessManager have been made private. Downstream code using these methods to configure service workers will need to be refactored, potentially using ServiceWorkerContextWrapperTestApi for test environments. (d0ba702)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature for the public API extensions::EventFilter::RemoveEventMatcher has been changed as part of a performance optimization. Call sites must be updated, and a new RemoveEventMatchers method is available for batch removals. (cf6d1ed)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method GetPersistentUsageRecordSessionSupport() has been removed from the media::KeySystemInfo interface. This will impact embedders implementing custom EME/CDM key systems. (acdadfe)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The should_show_inactive field has been removed from the public tabs::TabDialogManager::Params struct. Code using this parameter to show dialogs without activating the parent window must be updated. (ddc789c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The H.265 video decoder is now stricter and will generate a decoding error for streams with configuration changes (e.g., resolution) on non-key frames. This may break playback for previously working but technically invalid content. (aca89b5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature for BrowsingDataQuotaHelper::FetchResultCallback has changed. The callback now receives a std::vector<QuotaInfo> instead of a const std::list<QuotaInfo>&, requiring updates to implementations that query quota data. (0f27d66)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The command-line switch kEnableWebGLImageChromium is no longer available on Windows and macOS, as it has been restricted to ChromeOS. This removes a public flag used for specialized graphics overlay support. (9fccafe)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The method signature for the pure virtual function BnplUiDelegate::UpdateBnplIssuerUi has been changed. Custom implementations of this interface must be updated to match the new signature. (5cc1bba)

APIs & Services

  • The Direct Sockets API has been extended to support Source-Specific Multicast (SSM) for UDP sockets, allowing isolated web applications to join and leave multicast groups with source filtering. (0302377)
  • The TabStrip API has been extended with last_active_time_ticks and an experimental ReplaceTabInSplit method to better support features like Tab Search. (ce0f022)
  • A new set of UI pages and backend logic for "Actor Login M2" has been introduced to manage Gemini/AI-related login permissions. (1e06b17) (8ddb886) (59ffaad) (ad2c924) (f3d7714)
  • The webAuthenticationProxy API is now restricted to Windows, macOS, and Linux, removing it from other platforms. (5112a53)
  • The PageContentAnnotationsService has been refactored to integrate with PageEmbeddingsService via a new bridge, decoupling the components. (9eb352a)
  • The obsolete url_param_filter directory and its remaining code have been removed from the tree. (6af3e35)

Extensions

  • A crash has been fixed that occurred when guest content in a <webview> attempted to open a window with an unsupported disposition, such as Picture-in-Picture. (1e10f80)
  • A UI bug was fixed to ensure the extension side panel correctly receives focus when it is shown. (e7a534e)
  • The logic for opening extension options pages has been unified into a single helper function. (152d856)
  • For improved diagnostics, additional DumpWithoutCrash calls have been added to the extension service worker lifecycle to help debug initialization issues. (2a7a57d)

UI & UX

  • A new API, WebUILocationBar::GetAnchorOrNull, has been added to support upcoming UI chips that will appear in the location bar. (e2081ee)
  • On macOS, a bug where double-clicking the omnibox failed to select a word if the omnibox popup was visible has been fixed. (ac47a60)
  • The Side Panel infrastructure has been refactored to be more platform-agnostic, including renaming SidePanelUtil to SidePanelHelper. (f1041bd) (2af1630) (1ddac02)
  • Accessibility for radio buttons that are not direct siblings has been improved to ensure screen readers announce their group position and size correctly. (467d3bf)

Networking & Security

  • To reduce latency, the SQLite cookie store can now be initialized early upon creation rather than waiting for the first load request. (24ddf87)
  • Link Previews are now restricted to only http and https schemes for improved security. (57482f5)
  • Logic has been implemented to support Trust Anchor ID intersection for Multi-Certificate Transparency (MCT) in the TAI retry flow, enhancing connection security. (be0a703)
  • An old and unused cookie database migration path (from v22 to v23) has been removed, simplifying the codebase. (08708d4)

Graphics & Media

  • On Windows, the Dolby Vision decoder library is now preloaded in the GPU process to ensure it is available after the sandbox is engaged. (1fb8969)

Base, Testing & Infrastructure

  • base::RangeAsRvalues() now correctly supports reverse iteration if the underlying range is reversible. (49ea398)
  • In Headless mode on Windows, DevTools screen emulation methods (Emulation.setDeviceMetricsOverride and Emulation.clearDeviceMetricsOverride) are now supported. (18bc6e9)
  • The testing API for views has been enhanced with an explicit require_visible parameter in ElementTrackerViews and BrowserElementsViews, allowing tests to query for non-visible views. (c00788d)
  • views::ScrollView has new test-only methods, IsHorizontallyOverflowing() and IsVerticallyOverflowing(), to check for content overflow. (925ab27)

2026-03-09

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-03-09

Overview

This summary covers 521 commits from the main branch on March 9th, 2026, with a focus on changes relevant to developers building on Chromium. This digest highlights several breaking changes to core APIs for context menus, history, networking, and metrics, alongside improvements to UI, extensions, and performance. The first relevant commit is (704c022) and the last is (71c55fc).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public enum blink::WebMenuSourceType has been removed and replaced by ui::mojom::MenuSourceType. This directly impacts the public ContextMenuData struct, a core interface for context menu handling, and will require embedders to update their code to use the new type. (75a48e0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The HistoryService public API has been updated to use a strongly-typed history::ClusterId instead of a generic int64_t. This affects the signatures of public methods like ReplaceClusters and ReserveNextClusterIdWithVisit, requiring updates for any code that calls or implements them. (9cb1107)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ConnectionAllowlist mojo struct in services/network/public/mojom/ has been changed. It replaces bool fields for WebRTC connections and redirects with new enum-based fields. This is a signature change to a stable, public mojo interface that will break embedders who configure the network service. (9d5b5cf)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Core metrics APIs in base/metrics/ for histogram serialization have changed.
